description: An organized group of people living together with shared customs and institutions. 'Punoa' names the larger weave—the fabric of relationships that makes collective life possible.
sound_symbolism: The word gathers with structure. 'Pu' collects with gathering presence, people assembling. 'No' settles with organized form, structure emerging. Then 'a' opens to inclusive belonging.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo sila punoa norelu.' (The communities form a society.) Larger than 'shorai' (commune), the broad social fabric. Contains 'moenu' (culture) and 'keno' (custom).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Sangha writ large—society as extended community of practice and mutual support.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Society as conversation—social order maintained through ongoing dialogue and agreement.
